author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
originalBook: A Treatise on the Gift of Perseverance.
title: What Predestination is.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Will any man dare to say that God did not foreknow those to whom He would give to believe, or whom He would give to His Son, that of them He should lose none?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And certainly, if He foreknew these things, He as certainly foreknew His own kindnesses, wherewith He condescends to deliver us.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: This is the predestination of the saints,—nothing else; to wit, the foreknowledge and the preparation of God’s kindnesses, whereby they are most certainly delivered, whoever they are that are delivered.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But where are the rest left by the righteous divine judgment except in the mass of ruin, where the Tyrians and the Sidonians were left? who, moreover, might have believed if they had seen Christ’s wonderful miracles.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But since it was not given to them to believe, the means of believing also were denied them.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: From which fact it appears that some have in their understanding itself a naturally divine gift of intelligence, by which they may be moved to the faith, if they either hear the words or behold the signs congruous to their minds; and yet if, in the higher judgment of God, they are not by the predestination of grace separated from the mass of perdition, neither those very divine words nor deeds are applied to them by which they might believe if they only heard or saw such things.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Moreover, in the same mass of ruin the Jews were left, because they could not believe such great and eminent mighty works as were done in their sight.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For the gospel has not been silent about the reason why they could not believe, since it says: “But though He had done such great miracles before them, yet they believed not on Him; that the saying of Isaiah the prophet might be fulfilled which he spake, Lord, who hath believed our report, and to whom hath the arm of the Lord been revealed?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And, therefore, they could not believe, because that Isaiah said again, He hath blinded their eyes and hardened their heart, that they should not see with their eyes, nor understand with their heart, and be converted, and I should heal them.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Therefore the eyes of the Tyrians and Sidonians were not so blinded nor was their heart so hardened, since they would have believed if they had seen such mighty works, as the Jews saw.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But it did not profit them that they were able to believe, because they were not predestinated by Him whose judgments are inscrutable and His ways past finding out.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Neither would inability to believe have been a hindrance to them, if they had been so predestinated as that God should illuminate those blind eyes, and should will to take away the stony heart from those hardened ones.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But what the Lord said of the Tyrians and Sidonians may perchance be understood in another way: that no one nevertheless comes to Christ unless it were given him, and that it is given to those who are chosen in Him before the foundation of the world, he confesses beyond a doubt who hears the divine utterance, not with the deaf ears of the flesh, but with the ears of the heart; and yet this predestination, which is plainly enough unfolded even by the words of the gospels, did not prevent the Lord’s saying as well in respect of the commencement, what I have a little before mentioned, “Believe in God; believe also in me,” as in respect of perseverance, “A man ought always to pray, and not to faint.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For they hear these things and do them to whom it is given; but they do them not, whether they hear or do not hear, to whom it is not given.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because, “To you,” said He, “it is given to know the mystery of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it is not given.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Of these, the one refers to the mercy, the other to the judgment of Him to whom our soul cries, “I will sing of mercy and judgment unto Thee, O Lord.”
